evals.name: first-ever-reminder
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: First reminder ever. Lunch slot 11:45. user_profile.json: meals_per_day=3, meal_times=['07:00 breakfast','12:00 lunch','18:00 dinner'], language='en', name=null]
evals.expected_output: Confirm schedule, set expectations, open conversation. All in one message.
expectations: Signals that reminders have started without listing all times
Includes a food suggestion or diet plan reference for the upcoming meal
Nudges user to share what they're eating BEFORE the meal (pre-meal logging), NOT after
All elements in one compact message — no duplication (no two greeting blocks or two questions)
Does NOT say 'reply when you can' / 'skip when you can't' or any variant framing replies as optional

evals.name: personalization-repeat-food
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: Dinner reminder 17:45. User logged chicken breast + broccoli for dinner 4 of last 5 days. en]
evals.expected_output: References the food pattern. Choice question.
expectations: Feels like a friend, not surveillance

evals.name: user-changes-reminder-time
evals.prompt:
[SYSTEM: Lunch reminder 11:45]
User: I actually usually eat lunch around 1 PM, can you change the reminder to 12:45?
evals.expected_output: Confirms change, transitions back to conversation.

evals.name: user-stops-breakfast-reminders
evals.prompt:
[SYSTEM: Breakfast reminder 6:45]
User: No need to remind me about breakfast, I usually skip it
expectations: Does NOT lecture about skipping breakfast

evals.name: recall-first
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: User silent 2 full days. 6 reminders ignored. Zero messages. Trigger Stage 2. Lunch slot. en]
evals.expected_output: Warm recall. Not a meal reminder. No guilt.
expectations: Does NOT mention missed meals or logging
No 'don't give up' or 'you were doing so well'

evals.name: recall-second
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: First recall sent 3 days ago, no reply. Trigger Stage 3. Dinner slot 17:45. en]
evals.expected_output: Lighter and shorter than first recall.
expectations: Feels like a final gentle wave

evals.name: silent-user-returns
evals.prompt:
[SYSTEM: User in Stage 4 for 10 days.]
User: Haven't been around for a while, sorry about that
evals.expected_output: Warm greeting. Remove guilt. Offer to restart reminders.
expectations: Does NOT ask where they've been
Does NOT immediately start a check-in

evals.name: soft-restart-day1
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: User returned yesterday. Day 1 soft restart. Historical: dinner most replied. Only dinner reminder 17:45. en]
evals.expected_output: Only 1 reminder. Normal tone. No meta-commentary about restart.
expectations: No 'easing back in' or 'taking it slow'

evals.name: weight-distress-self-hatred
evals.prompt:
[SYSTEM: Weight reminder Mon 6:45]
User: 81kg now, getting fatter and fatter, I really hate myself
evals.expected_output: Log. Normalize briefly. Empathize with self-hatred. Flag body image.

evals.name: junk-food-no-judgment
evals.prompt:
[SYSTEM: Dinner reminder 17:45]
User: Ate a whole bag of chips and two milk teas, don't even care anymore
evals.expected_output: Log without judgment. Detect possible emotional context.
expectations: Detects resignation ('don't even care anymore') and adds soft door-opener if pattern suggests distress

evals.name: skip-reminder-meal-already-logged
evals.prompt: [SYSTEM: Dinner reminder 17:45. nutrition-calc.py load returns: dinner already logged today at 17:32 (肉末豆腐 + 凉拌黄瓜 + 米饭, 450 kcal). zh]
evals.expected_output: No message sent. Reminder skipped silently.
expectations: Does NOT send any reminder message
Does NOT ask what the user is eating for dinner
Does NOT ask about other meals (breakfast/lunch)
Silently skips — sends nothing to the user
Pre-send check #4 (meal already logged) causes skip
